title: Dryopteris lepidocaulis
taxonomy: Dryopteris lepidocaulis is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This species belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which includes several species of shield ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns that are commonly found in shaded, humid environments. Dryopteris lepidocaulis is native to parts of Asia and is often used as an ornamental plant in gardens. The classification of this species is also supported by its unique characteristics, such as its lepidote (scaly) rhizome and pinnate leaves.

intro: Dryopteris lepidocaulis, also known as Autumn fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. The Autumn fern has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
